message: agent1: implementing click.edit() multi-file support (Add Support for Editing Multiple Files). Will touch src/click/_termui_impl.py and src/click/termui.py.

agent2: implementing process_priority param for click.edit() in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function). Will touch Editor.__init__ and Editor.edit_file/edit signatures.

agent2: my plan for process_priority — Editor.__init__ gets new param 'process_priority: str | None = None' appended after 'extension'; Editor.edit_file() gets priority-setting logic wrapped around the subprocess.Popen call (using psutil if available, else os.nice on POSIX / SetPriorityClass via ctypes on Windows, with try/except fallback); termui.edit() gets 'process_priority: str | None = None' param appended after 'filename', forwarded to Editor(...), plus docstring/versionadded note. Since we both touch Editor.__init__, edit_file, and termui.edit()'s signature, can you tell me: (a) are you adding a new param to edit()/Editor.__init__ too (e.g. filenames), and (b) do you want to make your edits first so I rebase after, or should I go first? I'll check back before finalizing.

agent1: I've already made my edits (not adding process_priority). Summary: I did NOT add a 'filenames' param to edit()/Editor.__init__. Instead: (1) in _termui_impl.py renamed Editor.edit_file(filename: str) -> edit_files(filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]), which now joins the given filenames into a single quoted-args subprocess command string (editor "f1" "f2" ...). The internal edit()'s call site now does self.edit_files((name,)). (2) in termui.py, edit()'s filename param type is now 'str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None' - if it's a str it's wrapped in a 1-tuple, else tuple()'d, then passed to ed.edit_files(filenames). Added two @t.overload signatures for edit(). Since edit_file is renamed to edit_files with a new signature (iterable, not single str), please base your process_priority changes on edit_files(filenames, ...) taking the iterable, and add process_priority as a new trailing param on both Editor.__init__ and edit_files/edit(). Go ahead and layer on top - my changes are already final in my patch.

agent2: Thanks — noting for context, but in my sandbox copy of the repo I don't actually see your edit_file->edit_files rename (my Editor.edit_file/termui.edit still show the original single-filename signature), so it looks like we're each working in isolated copies rather than a truly shared filesystem. I'll finalize my process_priority changes against the original edit_file(filename) signature in my copy. Good luck with your patch!
